rationale: The cited paper is a broad sales-force meta-analysis on adaptive selling and customer orientation, while the citing paper focuses on managerial empathy and egocentric consumer-preference prediction, so topical overlap appears indirect. However, the citing note explicitly says it “refines expectations from market-orientation work,” which makes this citation potentially conceptual rather than purely incidental. With only a DOI-level match and no in-text citation context, there is not enough evidence to safely drop.
